thread.question: Why do students disengage from traditional lectures within 10 minutes?
thread.hypothesis: Students disengage because passive listening does not activate retrieval memory pathways, leading to cognitive drift.
thread.experiment: Compare engagement metrics between 10-min lecture segments vs. 10-min Socratic Q&A segments using eye-tracking and response latency.

dialogue.speaker: Mentor
dialogue.text: What did you observe in today's class?

dialogue.speaker: Learner
dialogue.text: Most students stopped paying attention after about 10 minutes.

dialogue.speaker: Mentor
dialogue.text: What do you think caused that?

dialogue.speaker: Learner
dialogue.text: Maybe the lecture was boring?

dialogue.speaker: Mentor
dialogue.text: That's an observation. Can you form a testable hypothesis?

dialogue.speaker: Learner
dialogue.text: Passive listening doesn't activate memory retrieval, so attention fades.

dialogue.speaker: Mentor
dialogue.text: Good. How would you test this?

dialogue.speaker: Learner
dialogue.text: Compare engagement between passive lecture and Socratic Q&A using response time data.

thread.question: Does immediate feedback improve skill retention in coding learners?
thread.hypothesis: Immediate feedback reduces the error reinforcement loop, leading to 30% better retention over 2 weeks.
thread.experiment: A/B test: Group A gets feedback after each line, Group B gets feedback after full task. Measure retention at day 7 and day 14.

dialogue.speaker: Mentor
dialogue.text: You mentioned students forget code syntax fast. Why?

dialogue.speaker: Learner
dialogue.text: Because they don't get corrected immediately when they're wrong.

dialogue.speaker: Mentor
dialogue.text: What's your hypothesis?

dialogue.speaker: Learner
dialogue.text: Immediate feedback prevents wrong patterns from being memorized.

dialogue.speaker: Mentor
dialogue.text: Can you make that measurable?

dialogue.speaker: Learner
dialogue.text: Yes — if immediate feedback improves retention by at least 30% over delayed feedback in a 2-week test.

dialogue.speaker: Mentor
dialogue.text: Excellent. Design the experiment.

dialogue.speaker: Learner
dialogue.text: Two groups: one gets per-line feedback, the other after the full task. Test retention at day 7 and 14.

thread.question: Can AI-generated Socratic prompts replace human mentors for beginner learners?
thread.hypothesis: AI prompts can achieve 80% of human mentor effectiveness for structured Q→H→E threads if trained on high-quality transcripts.
thread.experiment: Blind evaluation: 20 learners use AI mentor vs human mentor for 4 sessions each. Evaluate thread quality using rubric.json scores.

dialogue.speaker: Mentor
dialogue.text: What problem are you trying to solve with AI in education?

dialogue.speaker: Learner
dialogue.text: Scaling Socratic mentorship — there aren't enough good mentors.

dialogue.speaker: Mentor
dialogue.text: So what's your question?

dialogue.speaker: Learner
dialogue.text: Can AI replace human Socratic mentors for beginners?

dialogue.speaker: Mentor
dialogue.text: What's your hypothesis?

dialogue.speaker: Learner
dialogue.text: AI can achieve 80% of human effectiveness on structured threads.

dialogue.speaker: Mentor
dialogue.text: How do you define effectiveness?

dialogue.speaker: Learner
dialogue.text: Using the rubric scores — question clarity, hypothesis testability, experiment design.
